entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: The crop used in the participatory on-farm trials to assess grain yield.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The productivity measure of maize, affected by manure, nitrogen, and rainfall.

entities.name: MANURE
entities.description: Organic fertilizer applied at rates of 3 and 6 t ha-1 to improve maize yield.

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZER
entities.description: Chemical fertilizer (N) applied to increase maize grain yield.

entities.name: PARTICIPATORY ON-FARM TRIALS
entities.description: The research method involving farmer resource groups to assess agricultural inputs.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environmental climate characterized by low and variable rainfall.

entities.name: TSHOLOTSHO
entities.description: The specific district in Zimbabwe where the trials were conducted.

entities.name: ZIMBABWE
entities.description: The country where the research took place.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: The amount of precipitation received, which strongly influenced maize yields and response to manure.

entities.name: CATIONS
entities.description: Beneficial nutrients potentially provided by manure application.

entities.name: PHOSPHORUS
entities.description: A nutrient (P) potentially provided by manure application to benefit maize.

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER FARMS
entities.description: Small-scale agricultural operations targeted for productivity improvement.
